subtitle: A quiet public beach on Hawksnest Bay, St. John, that was the atomic-bomb physicist J. Robert Oppenheimer's Caribbean retreat — given to the island by his daughter Toni, who died here.
spike_statement: J. Robert Oppenheimer's Caribbean retreat, deeded to the people of St. John by his daughter Toni, who died here by suicide in 1977.

levers.photo_tone: turquoise Hawksnest Bay and coral-and-algae sand under sea-grape shade, a modest stone cottage at the tree line, the light bright over a quiet, half-forgotten beach
